natural_language_analysis.summary: Both return VCs are semantically valid. Each duplicated disjunct can be discharged by choosing the left branch. The pure insertion-order conjunct follows by unfolding xizi_insert_before_order. The spatial conjunct is reconstructed by unfolding xizi_cdll and choosing its existential endpoints and rest list; witness 2 additionally needs segment extension over prefix followed by old_prev and linklist_node_pre.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: Four points-to cells: root.next |-> node, root.prev |-> node, node.prev |-> root, and node.next |-> root.
vc_shape.pre_pure: root <> NULL, node <> NULL, old_prev = root, first = root, and old_order = [root].
vc_shape.post_exists: The xizi_cdll witnesses are first = node, prev = node, and rest = [node]; unfolding the singleton xizi_dllseg then uses next = root.

witnesses.witness_instantiation: Choose the left side of the duplicated disjunction. Unfold xizi_insert_before_order. Rewrite old_order to [root]. For xizi_cdll choose first=node, prev=node, rest=[node], and for the singleton segment choose next=root.
witnesses.space_plan: Unfold xizi_cdll and xizi_dllseg once. The root.next/root.prev cells satisfy the cdll root fields; the node.next/node.prev cells satisfy the singleton segment head; the nil segment tail reduces to root = root, node = node, and emp. Reassociate/commute separating conjunction and cancel all four cells.
witnesses.pure_plan: Use PreH5 to normalize the order to [root]; xizi_insert_before_order becomes reflexivity. PreH1 supplies root <> NULL and PreH2 supplies the non-null condition required by the singleton xizi_dllseg.
witnesses.refinement_plan: not applicable; this VC is a separation-logic return entailment with no safeExec state.
premise_discharge: all target points-to resources occur exactly once in the antecedent
witnesses.grouping_reason: Uses the same xizi_insert_before_order unfolding and xizi_cdll/xizi_dllseg reconstruction pattern as return_wit_2, in the singleton-prefix base case.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: root.next |-> first, root.prev |-> node, xizi_dllseg first root old_prev before_old_prev prefix, old_prev.next |-> node, old_prev.prev |-> before_old_prev, node.prev |-> old_prev, and node.next |-> root.
vc_shape.pre_pure: root <> NULL, node <> NULL, old_prev <> root, and old_order = root :: (prefix ++ [old_prev]).
vc_shape.pre_exists: Existentials internal to xizi_dllseg when prefix is nonempty.
vc_shape.post_exists: The xizi_cdll witnesses are first = first, prev = node, and rest = prefix ++ [old_prev; node].

witnesses.witness_instantiation: Choose the left side of the duplicated disjunction. Unfold xizi_insert_before_order. Rewrite old_order and list append so (root :: prefix ++ [old_prev]) ++ [node] is root :: (prefix ++ [old_prev; node]). Instantiate xizi_cdll with the existing first, new tail node, and that rest list.
witnesses.space_plan: Keep root.next and root.prev as the xizi_cdll root cells. Extend the existing xizi_dllseg over prefix with the two concrete nodes old_prev and node: old_prev has predecessor before_old_prev and successor node; node has predecessor old_prev and successor root. The resulting segment is xizi_dllseg first root root node (prefix ++ [old_prev; node]). This can be proved by induction on prefix or a group-local segment-extension helper, then spatial cancellation closes the goal.
witnesses.pure_plan: Unfold xizi_insert_before_order for reflexivity. Use PreH4 plus List.app_assoc/list simplification to normalize the target rest list. PreH2 provides node <> NULL. The validity/non-null fact needed when folding old_prev as a segment node is discharged from the owned old_prev.next points-to cell using the standard field points-to validity fact; this is also consistent with symbolic execution having safely dereferenced old_prev.
witnesses.refinement_plan: not applicable; this VC is a separation-logic return entailment with no safeExec state.

candidate_helper_declarations.statement_shape: For x px y py z q xs, xizi_dllseg x px y py xs ** y.next |-> z ** y.prev |-> py ** z.next |-> q ** z.prev |-> y |-- xizi_dllseg x px q z (xs ++ [y; z]), with z <> NULL supplied explicitly and y validity derived from its owned next-field cell.

premises.premise: the old tail y is a valid/non-null node when xizi_dllseg is folded
premises.discharged_by: the antecedent owns &(y->node_next) |-> z; use the standard points-to/field-validity fact inside the helper proof

premise_discharge: the existing segment and all four appended-node field cells are present in the antecedent
witnesses.failure_signal: none; helper proof route may be replaced by a direct induction over prefix without changing the specification
witnesses.grouping_reason: Same return postcondition and predicate unfolding as return_wit_1; this is the general nonempty-order segment-extension case and can be handled by the same worker context.

analysis_groups.shared_proof_pattern: Choose one identical disjunct, unfold the pure order relation, normalize append, unfold xizi_cdll, and reconstruct its xizi_dllseg from the post-update points-to cells.
analysis_groups.shared_space_plan: Witness 1 is the two-node base shape; witness 2 is the same construction after preserving the existing prefix segment and appending old_prev and node.
analysis_groups.shared_pure_plan: Order equalities from PreH5/PreH4 plus list append normalization; non-null root/node facts are explicit preconditions.
analysis_groups.grouping_reason: Both witnesses arise from the same function return and use one predicate/helper family; two witnesses fit below max_witnesses_per_group=12, so one group minimizes duplicated proof setup without mixing unrelated strategies.

candidate_helper_declarations.statement_shape: Extend xizi_dllseg x px y py xs by owned nodes y and z to xizi_dllseg x px q z (xs ++ [y; z]); add the current group suffix if materialized.

expected_helpers: Optional group-suffixed xizi_dllseg_append_two_nodes; a direct induction is also valid.
proof_groups.proof_strategy: Prove return_wit_1 first as the direct two-node circular-list construction, then reuse the unfolding/order-normalization pattern for return_wit_2 and prove the prefix segment extension by induction or a group-suffixed helper in case_lib. In both goals choose the left identical disjunct and never modify generated statements.

per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_insert_before_: Unfold xizi_insert_before_order, xizi_cdll, and singleton xizi_dllseg; instantiate node/node/[node] and cancel four cells.
Normalize old_order ++ [node], instantiate xizi_cdll with first/node/(prefix ++ [old_prev;node]), and extend the existing prefix segment with old_prev and node.

premise_discharge.old_tail_validity: owned old_prev.node_next points-to cell / standard field-validity fact
premise_discharge.order_shape: PreH5 for return_wit_1 and PreH4 plus app_assoc for return_wit_2

proof_groups.grouping_reason: Same function, same return postcondition, same xizi_cdll reconstruction, and one segment-extension helper family; group size 2 is within the controller limit 12.

controller_simple_repairs.reason: vc-checking report included an inline group_plan but the default group_plan.json artifact was missing

source_goal_version.digest_input_policy: relative_path+sha256+state+role over generated files and witness statements
